fragility_points: Lack of captured exact qualifying minute/timestamp in the artifact set.
Residual dependence on Polymarket API metadata accurately reflecting settlement mechanics.
key_assumptions: Polymarket's API-visible rule text matches the actual resolving contract.
Binance's reported 24-hour high above 2400 corresponds to a qualifying minute within the Apr 13-19 ET window.
main_logical_chain: Start from a high outside-view prior because a weekly ETH threshold modestly above spot is often reachable.
Check the exact contract wording because this is a rule-sensitive threshold market.
Find that the governing source of truth is Binance ETH/USDT 1-minute candle High during the stated ET window.
Verify same-venue Binance data shows a high above 2400, pushing the view from strong Yes to near-certain Yes.
main_thesis: This is effectively a Yes/already-met Binance threshold case rather than a narrative ETH call, because the governing venue-specific rule appears satisfied.
quote_anchors: resolve to 'Yes' if any Binance 1-minute candle for ETH/USDT ... has a final 'High' price equal to or greater than ... $2,400
source_quality_view: High-quality primary rules/state source plus high-quality same-venue verification; independence medium and source-of-truth ambiguity low after API check.
strongest_disconfirmers: The extra verification used Binance's 24-hour summary ticker rather than the exact 1-minute candle table named in the rules.
strongest_supports: Polymarket metadata explicitly defines the source of truth as Binance ETH/USDT 1-minute candle High and showed the market resolved Yes.
Binance public ticker reported ETHUSDT highPrice 2415.50, above the 2400 threshold.
timing_relevance: High: this is a narrow weekly window and the resolution depends on whether the threshold was hit during Apr 13-19 ET.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact first qualifying Binance minute candle was not independently archived in this run.
what_would_change_view: A Binance minute-candle audit showing no >=2400 High in-window, or a Polymarket correction/reversal, would materially change the view.
